headline: Berkshire’s New Era Under Greg Abel Housing Meets AI Investment Shift
summary: Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) is acquiring Taylor Morrison Home Corporation, marking the first major deal under new CEO Greg Abel. headline: Meta lashes Australia's bid to make tech giants pay for news
summary: Tech giant Meta on Thursday attacked Australia's "grossly unfair" bid to make social media companies pay for news, saying it is vehemently opposed to the draft laws."Our position is clear: this law is poorly designed, grossly unfair, and will fail to deliver a diverse and sustainable news industry," said Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram.

headline: UK Ruling On AI Search Puts Alphabet’s Publisher Relations In Focus
summary: The UK Competition and Markets Authority requires Google to let publishers opt out of AI-generated search summaries and training datasets. Google must provide clear attribution and links to publisher content within its AI search results. The ruling applies to Alphabet's (NasdaqGS:GOOGL) UK operations and includes testing new tools that give publishers more control. Alphabet, through Google Search, is heavily involved in building AI-powered search features that blend traditional links with...

headline: Google Stock Attracts Cathie Wood’s ARK and Berkshire Hathaway. That’s a Rarity.
summary: Cathie Wood and Warren Buffett couldn’t be more different when it comes to investing styles.  Wood’s ARK Invest is known for betting on disruptive tech like AI and robo-taxis, while Buffett spent his 55 years as  Berkshire Hathaway  CEO buying old-fashioned, reliable companies at fair prices.  Google parent Alphabet appears to be the rare stock that fits both strategies.

content.title: Google Stock Attracts Cathie Wood’s ARK and Berkshire Hathaway. That’s a Rarity.
content.summary: Cathie Wood and Warren Buffett couldn’t be more different when it comes to investing styles.  Wood’s ARK Invest is known for betting on disruptive tech like AI and robo-taxis, while Buffett spent his 55 years as  Berkshire Hathaway  CEO buying old-fashioned, reliable companies at fair prices.  Google parent Alphabet appears to be the rare stock that fits both strategies.

headline: The Dow Is Up 500 Points. Why the Nasdaq Is Sinking.
summary: The Dow opened higher, but the S&P 500 and Nasdaq were on pace to fall for a second day in a row. The Dow rose 503 points, or 1%. The S&P 500 fell 0.3%. The Nasdaq tumbled 1.1%. A majority of stocks in the Dow and S&P were rising, but the chip stock slide was overpowering the latter.

content.title: The Dow Is Up 500 Points. Why the Nasdaq Is Sinking.
content.summary: The Dow opened higher, but the S&P 500 and Nasdaq were on pace to fall for a second day in a row. The Dow rose 503 points, or 1%. The S&P 500 fell 0.3%. The Nasdaq tumbled 1.1%. A majority of stocks in the Dow and S&P were rising, but the chip stock slide was overpowering the latter.

headline: Broadcom downgraded as Google moves to reduce reliance on chip supplier
summary: Investing.com -- Investment bank Macquarie has downgraded Broadcom from Outperform to Neutral, citing growing concerns that key customer Google is increasingly developing its own artificial intelligence (AI) chip capabilities and diversifying suppliers. The firm also cut its 12-month price target on Broadcom by 15% to $437 per share, down from $513.

content.title: Broadcom downgraded as Google moves to reduce reliance on chip supplier
content.summary: Investing.com -- Investment bank Macquarie has downgraded Broadcom from Outperform to Neutral, citing growing concerns that key customer Google is increasingly developing its own artificial intelligence (AI) chip capabilities and diversifying suppliers. The firm also cut its 12-month price target on Broadcom by 15% to $437 per share, down from $513.

content.summary: If you have room for one mega-cap cloud name in a retirement portfolio right now, the question is simple: Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) or Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)? Both are minting cash off the AI buildout, but they offer very different risk and reward profiles. headline: Lovelace Matches Gemini Deep Research at Less Than 1% of the Cost
summary: As enterprises grapple with rising AI infrastructure costs, Lovelace today released benchmark results suggesting that better context, not bigger models, will be the next quantum leap in enterprise AI.

thumbnail.caption: Lovelace is the only provider of enterprise-scale context engines capable of analyzing trillions of data points to create knowledge graphs that are usable by autonomous agents at the speed, scale, and accuracy required for mission-critical analysis. (PRNewsfoto/Lovelace AI)

headline: Google Stock Attracts Cathie Wood’s ARK and Berkshire Hathaway. That’s Rare.
summary: Cathie Wood and Warren Buffett couldn’t be more different when it comes to investing styles.  Wood’s ARK Invest is known for betting on disruptive tech like AI and robo-taxis, while Buffett spent his 55 years as  Berkshire Hathaway  CEO buying old-fashioned, reliable companies at fair prices.  Google parent Alphabet appears to be the rare stock that fits both strategies.

content.title: Google Stock Attracts Cathie Wood’s ARK and Berkshire Hathaway. That’s Rare.

Wood’s ARK Invest is known for betting on disruptive tech like AI and robo-taxis, while Buffett spent his 55 years as  Berkshire Hathaway  CEO buying old-fashioned, reliable companies at fair prices.  Google parent Alphabet appears to be the rare stock that fits both strategies.

headline: Broadcom set to shed $300 billion in value as AI results fail to impress
summary: Broadcom shares slumped more than 14% on Thursday, dragging chip peers lower, after the company's results fell short of lofty expectations around demand for its custom AI chips ‌business.  Its crucial role in helping design in-house processors of firms like Alphabet and Meta that serve as alternatives to Nvidia's ​costly chips has made Broadcom one of the biggest winners of the AI boom.  Shares have climbed 38% this year, as of last close, including a 15% rally in the two weeks ahead of its earnings after rival Marvell Technology's strong results.
